data.title: Equities stall as early enthusiasm ebbs; Amazon, Apple earnings due
data.description:
Key points:
  • Microsoft, Meta rise after quarterly results
  • Semiconductor stocks weak
  • Economic data shows inflation rise, stable labor market

U.S. stocks closed well off early highs on Thursday, following the latest round of corporate earnings and economic data, as investors awaited results from megacaps Amazon and Apple due after the closing bell.

Microsoft shares rose after it posted a strong earnings report and briefly surpassed the $4 trillion market cap threshold , becoming only the second publicly traded company to ever touch the milestone after Nvidia .

Meta Platforms surged and hit an intraday record high of $784.75 as AI-driven growth in its core ad business powered a bullish revenue forecast.

Still, other AI-related names such as chipmakers Broadcom and Nvidia were weaker on the session, which weighed on the PHLX semiconductor index .

"Looking at the market action today, you have haves and have-nots, and so you have a couple tech companies, like a lot of the semiconductor-related and semi-cap equipment-related stocks are doing pretty poorly," said Ellen Hazen, chief market strategist at F.L. Putnam Investment Management in Lynnfield, Massachusetts.

"But then, of course, Microsoft is doing pretty well, and the same thing with Amazon and Meta, which are doing really well."

Of the 297 companies in the S&P 500 that have reported earnings through Thursday morning, 80.8% have topped analyst expectations, according to LSEG data, compared with the 76% beat rate over the past four quarters

According to preliminary data, the S&P 500 lost 19.33 points, or 0.37%, to end at 6,339.31 points, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 8.40 points, or 0.04%, to 21,138.08. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 320.83 points, or 0.72%, to 44,140.45.

The S&P 500 had risen as much as 1% and the Nasdaq as much as 1.5% earlier in the session. The Nasdaq has not logged a move of at least 1% in either direction since July 3 while the S&P last recorded a daily 1% move on June 24.

Earlier economic data from the Commerce Department report showed inflation picked up in June, with new tariffs pushing prices higher and stoking expectations that price pressures could intensify in the coming months, while weekly initial jobless claims signaled the labor market remained on stable footing.

Investors will now eye Friday's non-farm payrolls report and a looming tariff deadline, as U.S. President Donald Trump was expected to issue higher final duty rates for countries that have not reached an agreement, although Mexico was granted a 90-day reprieve.

U.S. stocks have rallied after a sharp selloff that began in early April after Trump announced a bevy of sharp tariffs, only to rebound as deals have been struck with many trading partners on duty levels. The Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq recorded their third straight monthly gain.

Drug stocks were also weaker after the White House said Trump sent letters to the CEOs of 17 major pharmaceutical companies, urging immediate action to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Americans. The NYSE Arca pharmaceutical index (.DRG) was down 2.3%.

data.title: SOUTHERN COPPER CORP/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Southern Copper Corporation, a leading mining company with operations primarily in Peru and Mexico, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting its ongoing commitment to sustainable development and operational efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Southern Copper Corporation reported the following key financial metrics for the three months ended June 30, 2025:

  • Net sales: $3,051.0 million, reflecting a slight decrease from $3,118.3 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Operating income: $1,587.0 million, compared to $1,607.3 million in the same period of 2024, indicating stable operational efficiency.
  • Net income: $976.7 million, showing an increase from $953.8 million in the same period of 2024, driven by improved interest income.
  • Net income attributable to SCC: $973.4 million, up from $950.2 million in the same period of 2024.
  • Net earnings-basic and diluted: $1.21 per share, consistent with the same period of 2024.

Business Highlights

Southern Copper Corporation's business performance for the second quarter of 2025 includes the following highlights:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates primarily in Peru and Mexico, producing copper and other minerals. Operations in Peru are conducted through a registered branch, while operations in Mexico are managed through subsidiaries.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates mining, smelting, and refining facilities in Peru and Mexico, with exploration activities in Argentina, Chile, Mexico, and Peru. The Peruvian operations are subject to audits by the Ministry of the Environment, and the company is in material compliance with applicable environmental laws.
  • Sales Units: Significant production volumes are maintained, with operations in Peru and Mexico contributing to the overall output. The company also maintains an asset retirement obligation for its mining properties in these regions, reflecting its commitment to environmental compliance.
  • New Production Launches: The company has expanded its Toquepala concentrator in Peru, which is part of its commitment to fund various social and infrastructure improvement projects in the region.
  • Future Outlook: Southern Copper Corporation is focused on integrating its operations with local communities to promote sustainable development. The company has committed to significant social and infrastructure projects in the Tacna, Moquegua, Apurimac, Arequipa, and Cajamarca regions of Peru. Additionally, the company is monitoring developments related to the implementation of Pillar Two tax regulations and the impact of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act on its operations.

SEC Filing: SOUTHERN COPPER CORP/ [ SCCO ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Climb Global Solutions, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Climb Global Solutions, Inc., a leading provider of technology solutions and services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant growth in both financial and operational metrics, driven by organic growth and strategic acquisitions. The company continues to expand its market presence and enhance its service capabilities, positioning itself for sustained future growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $297.3 million, increased 73% from the same period in the prior year due to organic growth and the acquisition of DSS.
  • Gross Profit: $49.7 million, increased 40% from the same period in the prior year, driven by organic growth and a $2.4 million contribution from the DSS acquisition.
  • Income from Operations: $12.7 million, reflecting a 64% increase compared to the same period in the prior year, supported by increased sales and effective cost management.
  • Net Income: $9.7 million, increased 57% from the same period in the prior year, with a net income margin of 3.2%.
  • Income per Common Share-Basic: $2.11, reflecting a 56% increase compared to the same period in the prior year, indicating strong earnings growth.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Distribution segment saw a significant increase in net sales, rising 74% to $153.0 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to the same period in the prior year. The Solutions segment also experienced growth, with net sales increasing by 48% to $6.3 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States remains the largest market, contributing $223.6 million in net sales for the six months ended June 30, 2025. Europe and the United Kingdom also showed strong performance, with net sales of $58.0 million, reflecting the company's expanding presence in these regions.
  • Customer Segments: The company had three major customers accounting for 24%, 20%, and 13% of total net sales during the three months ended June 30, 2025, indicating a concentrated customer base that significantly impacts sales performance.
  • Operational Metrics: Gross billings for the Distribution segment increased by 40% to $477.0 million, while the Solutions segment saw a 19% increase to $23.5 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025. This highlights the company's ability to drive sales volume across its segments.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth driven by its existing vendor partnerships and the integration of recent acquisitions, such as DSS. The focus will remain on expanding market share and enhancing customer service capabilities.
  • Acquisitions Impact: The acquisition of DSS contributed $2.4 million to gross profit for the three months ended June 30, 2025, underscoring the strategic importance of acquisitions in driving business growth.
  • SG&A Efficiency: Selling, general, and administrative expenses as a percentage of gross billings decreased to 3.3% for the three months ended June 30, 2025, from 3.6% in the prior year, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Vendor and Customer Incentives: Vendor rebates and discounts increased significantly to $5.5 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $1.6 million in the prior year, indicating enhanced vendor relationships and strategic pricing initiatives.

SEC Filing: Climb Global Solutions, Inc. [ CLMB ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: CULLEN/FROST BANKERS, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Cullen/Frost Bankers, Inc., a prominent financial institution based in Texas, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing robust financial and operational performance for the recent quarter. The report highlights significant growth in key financial metrics and provides insights into the company's business activities and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: $601.96 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, driven by higher interest income from loans and securities.

Net Interest Income: $429.60 million, showing an improvement due to a decrease in interest expense.

Net Income: $157.00 million, an increase from the previous year, indicating strong financial performance.

Net Income Available to Common Shareholders: $155.33 million, demonstrating a solid return for shareholders.

Basic EPS: $2.39, reflecting the company's ability to generate earnings per share growth.

Diluted EPS: $2.39, consistent with basic EPS, indicating no significant dilution from convertible securities.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

The report provides a breakdown of interest income from loans, securities, and deposits, highlighting the performance of different financial products. Interest income from loans, including fees, was a significant contributor, with notable income from taxable and tax-exempt securities.

Geographical Performance

Most of the lending activity occurs within Texas, particularly in major metropolitan areas such as Austin, Dallas/Ft. Worth, Houston, and San Antonio. The report notes that there are no significant concentrations of loans related to any single industry in excess of 10% of total loans, with the energy and automobile dealership industries being the largest.

Sales Units

The report details the volume of loans across various categories, including commercial and industrial, energy, commercial real estate, and consumer real estate. The total loans amounted to $21,254,495 as of June 30, 2025.

Future Outlook

The management discusses the economic conditions in Texas, using the Texas Leading Index as a measure, which suggests favorable economic conditions. The company continues to monitor credit quality indicators and economic conditions to manage its loan portfolio effectively.

SEC Filing: CULLEN/FROST BANKERS, INC. [ CFR ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025
